question_text: Which of the following correctly describes the relationship between the colour of light and its wavelength in the visible spectrum?

options.label: A
options.text: Red light has a shorter wavelength than blue light

options.label: B
options.text: Blue light has a longer wavelength than red light

options.label: C
options.text: Red light has a longer wavelength than blue light

options.label: D
options.text: All colours of visible light have the same wavelength

correct_answer: Red light has a longer wavelength than blue light
explanation: In the visible spectrum, different colours of light have different wavelengths. Red light has the longest wavelength (around 700 nm), while blue light has a shorter wavelength (around 450 nm). This is why red appears at one end of the rainbow and blue at the other.
